title: SOFI Stock Slides As Long-Term Growth Story Stays Intact
text: SoFi Technologies stock experienced a significant decline following Q1 2026 earnings despite strong operational results. The company delivered record revenue of $1.1 billion (up 41% YoY) and net income of $166.7 million, yet the stock fell approximately 13%. The disconnect between operational performance and stock price reflected investor concerns about valuation and near-term growth rates. Multiple analysts cited the company's failure to raise forward guidance despite beating quarterly expectations as a key disappointment. CEO Anthony Noto cited the Federal Reserve's expected rate freeze and macroeconomic uncertainty as reasons for maintaining conservative guidance. The company's guidance of 30% adjusted net revenue growth for 2026 appears conservative relative to the 41% growth achieved in Q1. Concerns about the technology platform segment's 27% YoY decline due to a customer departure also weighed on sentiment. However, the long-term growth story remains intact with record member additions (1.1 million in Q1), strong loan originations, and a clear path to sustainable profitability. The company's 10th consecutive quarter of GAAP profitability and improving EBITDA margins support confidence in long-term value creation, suggesting the stock's decline may represent a buying opportunity for long-term investors.
